sessions.summary: INC31707215 (Covestro, eu5-prod): activities via 'Get Opportunity data' (schema activity_opportunity, buId 4_Q3nyaIwT4MTfar5AshZKCw) frequently discarded ACTIVITY_DISCARDED_OUTDATED. Customer confused why a payload with later display time (06:18:20 vs 06:18:18) was discarded. Proved the rule from source (cdp-data develop): profilecombiner DefaultMutableActivityHandler.canMutateActivity mode Full discards only when STORED activityTime is strictly after incoming (equal = accepted). activityTime = ActivityDataDal.getActivityTime = UpdatedField ?? CreatedField, compared at full Instant sub-second precision. No activity-date field mapped -> created/updated default to ingestion instant, so ordering is by CDP receive-time not payload display time. Constant 23004, category Profile Merge, severity INFO. Kibana VPN-blocked so per-record activityTime proof not pulled; framed leading out-of-order-ingestion hypothesis as unverified. Handed off to data-infra (owns profilecombiner) with 4 asks; they own empirical proof + customer reply. Draft: Drafts/INC31707215-handoff-data-infra.md.

accomplishments: Updated the DoD default-on note so the doc matches what's being built:

sessions.summary: Shipped /wrap skill v2.1.0: compact-at-wrap. Added Step 0 (one-line heads-up at start that the wrap will run /compact at the end; default proceed, user-vetoable at any point) and a final Compact step that runs /compact after the Output Report unless vetoed. Added a Compact line to the Output Report format. Bumped CHANGELOG to 2.1.0 with the cost rationale: a long-lived single 'CDP investigator' session accrues cache-read cost with no residual value once /wrap persists findings to disk (cost-coach session 701acc40: ~58% of cost was cache-read from monotonic session growth). Thread 2 (INC31634525 investigation) explicitly dropped by user.
